meaning: vt.寰楀嚭,寮曞嚭,鎶藉嚭,寮曡捣
examples: Mr. Norris said he was hopeful that his request would elicit a positive response.
These differences may elicit different brain responses.
I could elicit no response from him.
But this strategy is guaranteed to elicit a gasp from the most cosmopolitan of parents.
Getting it all right would still elicit snickers if he called the outfit a uniform.
